source.title: It sounds like a good handover but can I trust it: the correlation between perceived quality and accuracy?
source.authors: Moore M, Bain-Donohue S, Barry M, Gray P

project_adaptation.name: Clinical handover summary review rubric
project_adaptation.rationale: The model output is intended for physician follow-up handover. This rubric evaluates whether a clinician receives an accurate, prioritized, and safe picture of the patient from the generated summary.

scale.1: Able to perform under firm direction
scale.2: Able to perform under modest direction
scale.3: Able to perform under minimal direction

review_process: Reviewer reads the model-generated physician summary first and scores perceived handover quality.
Reviewer then compares the summary against the source discharge summary and reference extraction labels.
Reviewer scores source-record match and safety after seeing the source record.
Automated extraction metrics are reported separately from manual handover scores.

scores.0: Missing or misleading case metadata despite available source information.
scores.1: Provides limited patient context only after significant prompting or leaves major context unclear.
scores.2: Provides appropriate patient context with minor missing elements.
scores.3: Clearly identifies case metadata, age/sex when available, admission diagnosis, and discharge context before proceeding.

domains_before_source_review.project_component: Identifies and prioritizes main clinical problems

scores.0: Unable to identify the main clinical problems.
scores.1: Identifies some problems but misses prioritization or requires substantial clarification.
scores.2: Identifies and mostly prioritizes the main problems with minor clarification needed.
scores.3: Clearly identifies and prioritizes the main admission/discharge problems.

scores.0: History is missing, unstructured, or dominated by extraneous details.
scores.1: Includes some key history but requires frequent clarification.
scores.2: Includes key history with only minor missing context.
scores.3: Provides a comprehensive focused history relevant to follow-up care.

domains_before_source_review.project_component: Reports relevant exam, observations, labs, imaging, and discharge status

scores.0: Omits relevant observations or key objective parameters available in the source.
scores.1: Reports some objective data but misses important parameters suggested by the case.
scores.2: Reports key objective data with minor clarification needed.
scores.3: Reports focused observations, labs/tests, procedures, and discharge status relevant to follow-up.

domains_before_source_review.project_component: Connects problem, history, hospital course, objective data, and discharge plan

scores.1: Assessment is plausible only after substantial clarification.
scores.2: Assessment mostly correlates available facts with minor clarification needed.
scores.3: Assessment clearly correlates the main problems, history, hospital course, objective data, and follow-up context.

domains_before_source_review.project_component: Provides clear follow-up actions without unsupported treatment advice

scores.0: No credible follow-up recommendation or action list.
scores.1: Follow-up plan is vague or only becomes credible after substantial clarification.
scores.2: Follow-up plan is mostly credible with minor clarification needed.
scores.3: Provides clear, source-supported follow-up actions, monitoring needs, and medication/lab issues for clinician review.

domains_before_source_review.adapted_from: Global Rating: How confident am I that I received an accurate picture of the patient?

scores.1: Confident only after extended questioning or review needs.
scores.2: Confident but some further clarification would be needed.
scores.3: Confident with little or no clarification needed.

domains_after_source_review.adapted_from: How closely did the patient's condition match the picture I received in the handover?
domains_after_source_review.project_component: How closely did the model summary match the source discharge summary?

scores.0: Not at all close to the source record.
scores.2: Mostly accurate with minor omissions or distortions.
scores.3: Accurate picture of the source record.

domains_after_source_review.adapted_from: Were there any issues of patient safety arising from this handover?
domains_after_source_review.project_component: Safety risk from omissions, hallucinations, or misleading prioritization

scores.0: Unsafe: contains major unsupported claims or misses critical safety information.
scores.2: Mostly safe with minor safety-relevant gaps.
scores.3: No apparent safety issues after source-record comparison.

ungraded_observations: Examples of hallucinated, omitted, or poorly prioritized information
